AMZN.reasoning: Vote count: 1 bullish (swing_trend_momentum, 62), 1 bearish (swing_mean_reversion, 62), 3 neutral (swing_breakout 30, swing_catalyst_news 55, swing_macro_context 68). The trend is unambiguously real — daily EMA stack is clean, ADX at 61.85 is an exceptionally strong reading, and 21-day ROC of +30.28% confirms a major momentum leg. However, four of five agents are aligned on the same core message: this setup is NOT actionable today. The reasons stack: (1) Q1 2026 earnings are due April 30 after close — one trading day away — creating a binary event that invalidates standard swing entry math. A miss or guidance softness from AWS at RSI 86+ would gap through all technical support levels. (2) Daily RSI-14 at 86.14 and RSI-21 at 89.35 are at extreme historical overbought readings. Price is 17.46% above the 50-SMA with a z-score of 2.0 — the stock is running two standard deviations extended. (3) Hourly timeframe shows early distribution: OBV is diverging bearishly from price, hourly MACD histogram is negative (-0.48), hourly ADX minus_di (27.61) exceeds plus_di (18.37), and volume bias is down. Sellers are absorbing into strength on the intraday chart. (4) The swing_macro_context agent ran explicit R/R math: best-case upside to $282 vs stop at $253 yields only 1.9:1, below the 2:1 minimum bar — and the earnings binary compresses this further. (5) swing_breakout confirms the original $199-$249 consolidation breakout is fully mature with a measured target of $299 still in play, but Bollinger width at 0.321 signals the volatility expansion phase is already done — not a fresh coiling setup. MACRO VETO APPLIED: swing_macro_context explicitly flags Amazon earnings as a primary regime risk-off trigger this week. Per head trader rules, macro veto carries override weight when the regime read is strong. Even if trend_momentum and mean_reversion were to resolve bullishly, the binary event alone is sufficient to block a new swing entry. BEST FORWARD SETUP: Post-earnings re-entry. If Q1 beats and AWS guidance is strong, the stock may gap up then consolidate — a clean RSI reset toward 60-70 with a re-test of the $253-258 EMA zone would be the high-conviction entry for the continuation leg toward the $282-299 measured target range. If earnings disappoint, the swing_macro_context re-entry zone of $240-$248 becomes the target.
AMZN.key_conflicts: The principal conflict is between swing_trend_momentum (bullish, 62) and swing_mean_reversion (bearish, 62) — but this disagreement is actually informative rather than paralyzing. Trend_momentum says 'the daily trend is genuinely strong and should be respected — this is a pullback-to-EMA continuation setup, not a top.' Mean_reversion says 'RSI 86+ with a z-score of 2.0 is a statistical extreme that historically precedes snapbacks, and the hourly chart is already confirming distribution.' Critically, both agents cite the SAME hourly evidence (OBV divergence, MACD histogram negative, minus_di > plus_di) and both acknowledge the extreme RSI — they simply weight it differently. Trend_momentum is waiting for a pullback to the 10 EMA (~$253) to enter long; mean_reversion is already short at current price targeting the middle Bollinger Band (~$239). This exact configuration — trend extended, reversal risk rising, both cases supported by same data — is precisely what the swing_breakout agent's neutrality and swing_macro_context's explicit R/R calculation resolve: the setup is not yet favorable in either direction given the imminent binary event. The earnings wildcard (April 30) makes both the trend-continuation entry and the mean-reversion fade higher-risk than they appear on the chart alone. Post-earnings, one of these two agents will be clearly right — and that is the moment to act.

synthesis_notes.earnings_veto: Q1 2026 earnings April 30 after close — binary event veto applied per macro and catalyst agents
synthesis_notes.re_entry_zone_bearish: 240.0-248.0 if earnings disappoint and AWS growth decelerates
synthesis_notes.macro_regime: transitional / risk-on but fragile — Fed hold, earnings season binary risk, Iran tail risk active
